disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A severe flare of long-standing low back pain began three days ago after minor exertion (gardening), on a background of an 18-month history of pain following a lumbar injury
trigger_question_examples: What has been happening with your pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It flared up three days ago after I did some gardening. My back has been bad for about 18 months since I hurt it, but this is far worse than usual.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Complete insomnia - zero sleep over three consecutive nights - with profound fatigue and exhaustion
trigger_question_examples: How are you coping with this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I have not slept a single hour in three nights. I am completely wrecked. The pain will not let me rest.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound low mood, despair, tearfulness and hopelessness that the patient links to the severity of the pain flare and sleep loss
trigger_question_examples: How are you feeling in yourself through all this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I feel completely hopeless. I have not stopped crying. I have shut myself away from everyone.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: On direct, sensitive questioning the patient discloses thoughts that he cannot go on and cannot keep himself safe because of the pain
trigger_question_examples: Sometimes when pain is this bad and sleep is gone, people have thoughts of not wanting to go on, or of harming themselves. Have you had any thoughts like that?
Have you had thoughts that you would be better off not here, or of ending your life?
Do you feel able to keep yourself safe tonight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: If you are asking straight... yes. I keep thinking I cannot do this any more, that I cannot keep myself safe if it stays like this. I just want it to stop.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: It is just the pain talking, I do not really mean anything by it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Escalating, unsupervised over-the-counter analgesic use - taking well above label amounts of paracetamol and ibuprofen over three days without relief; multiple part-used bottles at the bedside
trigger_question_examples: Exactly how much paracetamol and ibuprofen have you taken, and over what time?
How many tablets have you taken today and yesterday?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I have honestly lost count. I have been taking handfuls of paracetamol and ibuprofen for three days trying to get on top of it. There are a few bottles by the bed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new leg weakness, no numbness around the buttocks/genitals (saddle area), and no loss of bladder or bowel control
trigger_question_examples: Any new weakness in your legs, or numbness around your back passage or genitals?
Any trouble controlling your bladder or bowels?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that. My legs still work fine and I have no trouble with the toilet. It is just the pain and I cannot sleep.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Regular prescribed neuropathic pain medications (pregabalin and low-dose amitriptyline at night) are being taken as usual
trigger_question_examples: What medications are you on regularly?
What do you normally take for the nerve pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I take pregabalin during the day and a small amitriptyline tablet at night, and I have kept taking them as normal.
